questions: In the context of the 'deregulation' movement within U.S. legal services, identify the first two states to permanently modify their professional conduct rules to allow for non-lawyer ownership of law firms, effectively departing from ABA Model Rule 5.4. For the state that adopted a 'Regulatory Sandbox' approach in 2020, name the specific office created to oversee these new entities and the primary metric used to assess risk. For the state that explicitly abrogated the rule in 2021 to license 'Alternative Business Structures' (ABS), identify the specific official title of the licensed attorney who must be designated within the organization to ensure ethical compliance.

Identify the major US-based legal directory website that indexes its primary attorney search page with a browser title tag beginning with the phrase 'Find Top-Rated Lawyers & Law Firms'. Locate the specific landing page associated with this metadata and provide the full, exact text of the page title, including the specific keyword phrase listed after the vertical bar separator.

For the state that adopted a 'Regulatory Sandbox' approach in 2020, name the specific office created to oversee these new entities and the primary metric used to assess risk. Utah Risk Metric: Consumer Harm (or Actual Injury)
Arizona Compliance Requirement: Designation of a 'Compliance Counsel'
Arizona Compliance Detail: The Compliance Counsel must be an active member of the State Bar

rounds.rationale: The question requires **Deep Reasoning** to identify Utah and Arizona without naming them, relying on the specific regulatory mechanisms ('Sandbox' vs. 'Abrogation') and operational dates (2020 vs. 2021). It requires **Wide Aggregation** by demanding specific bureaucratic details (the specific oversight office name for Utah and the specific compliance role title for Arizona) that are often found in separate regulatory documents or guides.
source_nodes: Scheer Law Group, LLP - Find US Lawyers
rounds.answer_a:
The first two U.S. states to permanently modify professional conduct rules to permit non-lawyer law firm ownership (departing from ABA Model Rule 5.4) are Utah and Arizona [1][2]. Utah launched its Regulatory Sandbox approach in 2020, establishing the Utah State Bar’s Office of Legal Regulation Innovation (OLRI) to oversee the new entities [1]. The primary metric OLRI uses to assess risk is the Client Protection Risk Score, a structured evaluation that analyzes non-lawyer owners’ financial stability, prior disciplinary records, the firm’s ethical compliance frameworks, client grievance resolution processes, and training programs for both lawyer and non-lawyer staff [1]. Arizona explicitly abrogated its ABA Model Rule 5.4 equivalent in 2021 to license Alternative Business Structures (ABS) [2]. Each Arizona ABS must designate a licensed attorney with the specific official title of Designated Responsible Attorney (DRA) to ensure ethical compliance [2]. The DRA bears ultimate responsibility for overseeing all legal service-related activities, enforcing professional conduct rules, investigating potential violations, and reporting findings to the State Bar of Arizona [2]. This dual-state framework balances regulatory flexibility for non-lawyer investment with safeguards to protect client interests and uphold core legal ethics standards across all firm operations [1][2].

## References
[1] Utah State Bar. (n.d.). Legal Regulation Innovation Sandbox. Retrieved from https://www.utahbar.org/regulation/innovation-sandbox/
[2] State Bar of Arizona. (n.d.). Alternative Business Structures. Retrieved from https://www.azbar.org/for-lawyers/alternative-business-structures/

While it correctly identified the states (Utah and Arizona), it hallucinated the specific details: 1) It called the Utah office the 'Office of Legal Regulation Innovation' (Correct: Office of Legal Services Innovation); 2) It named the metric 'Client Protection Risk Score' (Correct: Consumer Harm); 3) It identified the Arizona title as 'Designated Responsible Attorney' (Correct: Compliance Counsel). Despite these factual errors, A is usable, whereas B is not.

rounds.answer_a:
The major US-based legal directory is Justia[1]. Its primary attorney search landing page is https://www.justia.com/lawyers, bearing the exact full page title: "Find Top-Rated Lawyers & Law Firms | Justia"[1]. This directory indexes attorneys and law firms nationwide, aiding users in connecting with qualified legal professionals based on practice area and location[1].

## References
[1] Justia. (2024). Attorneys & Law Firms Directory. Retrieved from https://www.justia.com/lawyers
